refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with communication quality meeting the customer-defined packet loss limit.
refined_fr.rationale: The candidate FR is already focused on a single functional obligation: ensuring teleoperation communication quality meets a specified packet loss limit. It does not combine multiple requirements or include implementation details, thus it is atomic and solution-neutral.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the packet loss rate during teleoperation under agreed network conditions and verify it does not exceed the customer-defined limit.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with communication quality meeting the customer-defined packet loss limit.

candidate_dps: Implement a communication protocol with adaptive error correction and retransmission mechanisms to maintain packet loss within limits
Design a network quality monitoring and control system that dynamically adjusts transmission parameters to meet packet loss targets
Use a redundant communication path strategy to ensure packet loss remains below the threshold
Incorporate a buffering and smoothing mechanism at the receiver to compensate for packet loss and maintain teleoperation quality
selected_dp.selected_dp: Design a network quality monitoring and control system that dynamically adjusts transmission parameters to meet packet loss targets

potential_sources: Tight integration between monitoring and control modules could create coupling if not properly modularized
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically separates monitoring from control actions, supporting independence if implemented with clear interfaces.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions are within agreed parameters and can be monitored accurately
Risk that dynamic adjustments may introduce latency affecting teleoperation responsiveness
Assumes that operational policies allow for dynamic control of communication parameters
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Clarify the scope of network conditions and define interface boundaries between monitoring and control to ensure modularity and reduce coupling.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses network-related functionalities including monitoring and control of network parameters to meet performance targets.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Used to receive network performance data and send monitoring reports.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interface to adjust transmission parameters and receive network status updates.

module_containment_scores.notes: Highly cohesive as network quality monitoring and control logically belong to NGHE Solution; minimal diffusion expected outside this module.
Vehicle module is more about physical vehicle functions; network control would be diffused and less cohesive here.
Teleoperation depends on network quality but does not own network control; partial containment but moderate diffusion.
Safety Services focus on safety alerts and monitoring, not network control; poor containment and high diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary to contain the network quality monitoring and control DP, as it specializes in network-related functions and can encapsulate the dynamic adjustment of transmission parameters with minimal diffusion.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on Telemetry and Network Link interfaces may require careful interface management to avoid tight coupling.
If network control logic overlaps with Teleoperation functions, coordination mechanisms will be needed to prevent conflicting adjustments.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP logically addresses the FR by specifying a network quality monitoring and control system that dynamically adjusts transmission parameters to meet the packet loss target, directly supporting teleoperation communication quality requirements.

diffusion_risks: Potential dependency on Telemetry and Network Link interfaces may require careful interface management to avoid tight coupling.
If network control logic overlaps with Teleoperation functions, coordination mechanisms will be needed to prevent conflicting adjustments.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high containment (score 9) and low diffusion (score 2), indicating that the DP is well encapsulated within this IM boundary. Other modules show poor containment and higher diffusion, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the implementation module.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through monitoring packet loss metrics via the Telemetry interface and validating that packet loss remains within the customer-defined limit under agreed network conditions.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the NGHE Solution IM is clear and appropriate, with well-defined interfaces (Telemetry and Network Link) supporting the required functionality.

recommended_revisions: Explicitly define coordination mechanisms between NGHE Solution and Teleoperation modules to prevent conflicting network control actions.
Detail interface contracts for Telemetry and Network Link to minimize coupling and clarify data exchange protocols.
Include verification procedures in the NGHE Solution module to continuously monitor packet loss and trigger adjustments as needed.

decision.justification: Although all gates have passed indicating that the current DP and IM adequately address the FR, the high safety criticality and identified diffusion risks necessitate explicit definition of coordination mechanisms and detailed interface contracts to prevent potential conflicts and tight coupling. Additionally, verification procedures should be incorporated to ensure continuous monitoring and control of packet loss. These revisions will strengthen the design's robustness and safety assurance before proceeding to further decomposition.

revise.why: To explicitly define coordination mechanisms between NGHE Solution and Teleoperation modules, detail interface contracts for Telemetry and Network Link to minimize coupling, and include verification procedures for continuous monitoring and adjustment of packet loss.

assumptions: The current DP logically satisfies the FR as a network quality monitoring and control system.
The NGHE Solution module is the appropriate IM with high containment and low diffusion scores.
open_risks: Potential dependency on Telemetry and Network Link interfaces may cause tight coupling if not managed.
Possible conflicting network control actions between NGHE Solution and Teleoperation modules without coordination mechanisms.
evidence_needed: Detailed interface contracts for Telemetry and Network Link.
Defined coordination mechanisms between NGHE Solution and Teleoperation modules.
Verification procedures and monitoring metrics for packet loss within NGHE Solution module.
